operation: First, a workplane is initialized on the XY plane. A centered rectangle with X dimension 5 and Y dimension 10 is sketched, then extruded 4 units along the positive Z axis to create the lower base block. Next, the top positive Z face of the base block is selected, and a new workplane is created on this face, with its origin offset 5 units along the X axis. A second centered 5x10 rectangle is sketched at this offset origin, then extruded 4 units along the positive Z axis to form the raised upper block, completing the model.
geometry: This is a stepped rectangular solid composed of two adjacent equal-width cuboids aligned along their shared long axis. The left half of the solid along the length direction has a lower height, while the right half has twice the height of the left section, with the two cuboids joined edge-to-edge to form a single continuous L-shaped profile when viewed along the width axis.
function: This stepped block is designed as a height-adjusting spacer or mounting base, intended to support two adjacent components at different vertical elevations while keeping them consistently aligned laterally across the full width of the part.
